findings.evidence: Complex.lean:62 defines `transposeEquiv` via `Function.Involutive.toPerm`; Complex.lean:86-88 has `rw [Finsupp.domLCongr_symm]; rfl`; Mathlib/Logic/Equiv/Basic.lean:779 provides `Function.Involutive.toPerm_symm`.
findings.fix: After `rw [Finsupp.domLCongr_symm]`, rewrite with `Function.Involutive.toPerm_symm` instead of ending with bare `rfl`.
findings.issue: `transposeEquiv_symm` now closes by `rfl` after `Finsupp.domLCongr_symm`, relying on definitional equality of `(Function.Involutive.toPerm _ GridState.transpose_transpose).symm`.

findings.evidence: Complex.lean:96 defines `rotateEquiv` via `Function.Involutive.toPerm`; Complex.lean:120-122 has `rw [Finsupp.domLCongr_symm]; rfl`; Mathlib/Logic/Equiv/Basic.lean:779 provides `Function.Involutive.toPerm_symm`.
findings.fix: Use the named theorem `Function.Involutive.toPerm_symm` after `Finsupp.domLCongr_symm`, matching the mathematical reason the inverse permutation is itself.
findings.issue: `rotateEquiv_symm` has the same undocumented definitional-equality dependency for `(Function.Involutive.toPerm _ GridState.rotate_rotate).symm`.

summary: The changed definitions make the existing symmetry proofs depend on `Function.Involutive.toPerm`'s reducible structure instead of the named API. Replace the bare `rfl` closures with the explicit `Function.Involutive.toPerm_symm` rewrite.
